reasons: Khufra is the strongest direct counter because Bouncing Ball punishes blink movement.
Benedetta's core threat is dash and blink chaining, so Khufra directly attacks the center of her kit.

proof.summary: Benedetta wants to dash across fights, while Khufra's Bouncing Ball knocks airborne enemies who try to blink across him.
worksBestWhen: Khufra positions between Benedetta and the backline.
Khufra saves Bouncing Ball for her real entry.
failureCases: Benedetta waits out the anti-dash window.

reasons: Obsidia's Hunt of Bone is a strong direct answer because it can disable displacement skills during the tether.
This is especially relevant against Benedetta, whose safety and damage pattern depend heavily on dash access.

proof.summary: Benedetta wants to escape and reposition through dash skills, while Obsidia can tether her with Hunt of Bone and disable displacement skills during the punish window.
worksBestWhen: Obsidia lands Hunt of Bone after Benedetta commits or after An Eye for An Eye is unavailable.
Obsidia has enough follow-up damage or team support during the tether.
failureCases: Benedetta blocks the setup with correct control-immunity timing.
Obsidia tethers Benedetta in a bad position and gets dragged into danger.

reasons: Minsitthar can restrict Benedetta's dash options if she is caught inside King's Calling.
This is strong but more placement and timing dependent than Khufra or Obsidia.

proof.summary: Benedetta wants to chain dashes through a fight, while Minsitthar's King's Calling prevents directional blink skills inside the field.
worksBestWhen: The team follows up while Benedetta is trapped in the zone.
failureCases: Benedetta uses immunity timing before the trap is meaningful.

reasons: Kaja can suppress Benedetta after she commits, ignoring her normal mobility-based escape pattern.
His drag makes the punish window easier for teammates to convert.

proof.summary: Benedetta wants to enter and escape through dash timing, while Kaja can suppress and drag her during a committed window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits for Benedetta to spend mobility.
Allies are ready to burst during suppression.
failureCases: Benedetta blocks the setup before suppression lands.

reasons: Franco can suppress Benedetta with Bloody Hunt if she enters his range.
The counter is valid but more execution-dependent than Kaja because Franco needs accurate timing and can be outmaneuvered.

proof.summary: Benedetta wants to dodge normal control and escape after committing, while Franco can suppress her if he catches her outside her defensive timing.
worksBestWhen: Franco waits until Benedetta's An Eye for An Eye is unavailable or mistimed.
Franco suppresses after Benedetta commits forward.
failureCases: Franco wastes hook or ultimate timing.
Benedetta blocks the setup with control-immunity timing.
